rag_rules: Use assign only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable in implementation scope
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex; otherwise remove the guarantee
AGREE scalar declaration types are int, real, and bool
Align helper declaration types with the expression; comparison expressions are bool, integer arithmetic is int, and real arithmetic is real
generic_rules: Trace the first unresolved identifier or invalid helper declaration on the same line when AGREE reports a type error
Fix the root unresolved reference, or delete the invalid clause instead of adding architecture
Use only visible identifiers in the current scope to avoid reference resolution errors
Align data types in assignments and comparisons to avoid type mismatch errors
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use assign for implementation-level signal routing when the left-hand side is an existing output feature
Use eq helpers for complex expressions and comparisons in both component type and implementation contracts
Use const for constant values that are used in multiple expressions
anti_patterns: Do not use package-qualified identifiers, data types, or property references in AGREE contracts
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
